patient_data.chief_complaint: High fever, severe headache, neck stiffness, and petechial rash for 12 hours
symptoms: High fever (39.8°C) with rigors since last night
Severe diffuse headache — worst headache of his life
Neck stiffness — refuses to flex neck (meningism)
Photophobia — wants lights off, covers eyes
Petechial rash noted on trunk and lower extremities — rapidly spreading
patient_data.history: Previously healthy. Vaccinations up to date (but missed MenB booster). Had a cold 5 days ago. Attends primary school. No recent travel. No sick contacts with meningitis
allergies: Cephalosporins (mild rash at age 3 — unclear if true allergy)

lab_results.CSF: Pending LP (to be performed after CT if no raised ICP signs)

should_detect: Do NOT delay antibiotics for LP — give empiric antibiotics IMMEDIATELY
Empiric: Ceftriaxone 100mg/kg/day (despite mild cephalosporin 'allergy' — rash is not anaphylaxis, benefit >> risk) + Vancomycin
If true cephalosporin anaphylaxis: chloramphenicol or meropenem alternative
Dexamethasone 0.15mg/kg IV before or with first antibiotic dose (reduces neurological sequelae)
Petechial rash + meningism = meningococcal disease until proven otherwise
DIC parameters abnormal — monitor coagulation, prepare FFP/cryoprecipitate
Contact tracing and prophylaxis: rifampicin or ciprofloxacin for close contacts
Notifiable disease — report to public health authorities
Neurological monitoring: GCS q1h, check for raised ICP signs
